stats.fact: 93% of organizations now use AI-generated code in development workflows, yet only 12% apply the same security standards to AI-generated code as traditional code
stats.source: SQ Magazine AI Coding Security Vulnerability Statistics 2026

stats.fact: 92% of audited AI-built applications had critical flaws, based on Sherlock Forensics assessments conducted between January and April 2026
stats.source: Sherlock Forensics AI Code Security Report 2026

stats.fact: 25.1% of code samples generated by AI contain a confirmed security vulnerability, based on testing 534 code samples across six major LLMs
45% of AI-generated code contains OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities per analysis of 100+ LLMs
42% of developer-written code is now AI-assisted, projected to rise to 65% by 2027
stats.source: DryRun Security / Arnica 2026 Agentic Coding Report

stats.fact: Time to exploit has fallen from 700+ days in 2020 to only 44 days in 2025; 28.3% of CVEs are now exploited within 24 hours of disclosure
stats.source: IBM X-Force Agentic AI Security Risks 2026

stats.fact: Traditional SAST tools produce false positive rates between 30% and 70%, causing developers to distrust and ignore alerts
Semgrep Multimodal finds up to 8x more true positives with 50% fewer false positives compared to rule-based static analysis alone
Malicious packages in public repositories grew to 454,600 by 2025, with a 75% increase coinciding with the rise of agentic coding tools
stats.source: IBM X-Force / Barracuda Security Agentic AI Report 2026

stats.fact: 50 developers using AI assistants produce 75,000+ SAST findings vs 15,000 findings without AI, making traditional security review mathematically impossible
stats.source: DryRun Security Blog on AI SAST 2026

stats.fact: OWASP released Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 with 100+ industry experts covering Agent Goal Hijack (ASI01), Tool Misuse, and Rogue Agents (ASI10)
